nuggets.headline: Billie Eilish recorded this entire album in her brother's bedroom studio
nuggets.text: This track, and the whole of "WHEN WE ALL FALL ASLEEP, WHERE DO WE GO?", was co-written and produced by Billie Eilish and her brother Finneas O'Connell in his small bedroom in their parents' Highland Park home. Using a minimal setup of Apple's Logic Pro, a Universal Audio interface, and Yamaha monitors, Eilish often recorded her vocals while sitting on Finneas's bed, giving the album its signature intimacy.

source.title: Why Billie Eilish's When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go? is a modern masterpiece
source.quoteSnippet: This was not one of those 'bedroom studios' which was secretly more tantamount to a pro studio, but an*actual*bedroom with a bed... Eilish would sit on the bed to record her vocals.

nuggets.imageCaption: Billie Eilish in 2019, the year "bad guy" redefined the pop landscape.

nuggets.headline: The primary synth hook in 'bad guy' is a recording of a Sydney traffic signal
nuggets.text: While on a walk in Sydney, Australia, Billie Eilish was struck by the sound of a pedestrian crosswalk signal. She recorded the distinctive 'doop, doop' sound on her phone, and her brother Finneas later polished the sample and incorporated it as a key rhythmic and melodic element in the track. It's a piece of the everyday urban environment transformed into a Grammy-winning hook.

source.title: Billie Eilish says her Grammy-winning song 'Bad Guy' used sound samples from a traffic signal in Sydney – Firstpost
source.quoteSnippet: In a 2019 interview with Rolling Stone, she had revealed that the song sampled the sound that pedestrian crossing signals make... Eilish says she recorded the sound on her phone.

nuggets.headline: If you hear the cinematic scope of Lana Del Rey in Billie Eilish's work, you're hearing a direct influence
nuggets.text: The dark, atmospheric, and genre-blending style of "WHEN WE ALL FALL ASLEEP, WHERE DO WE GO?" draws from clear influences, including the work of Lana Del Rey. Eilish and Finneas built upon that foundation of cinematic pop to create a sound that is wholly their own. For a taste of that musical lineage, start with Del Rey's 2012 album "Born to Die."

source.title: “Ironic, self-aware, intimate, DIY... a song that builds power as it unfolds”: The making of Bad Guy, Billie…
source.quoteSnippet: The album showcased an artist of exceptional talent, one who drew on clear influences such as Lana Del Ray and Lorde and yet created a sound and a style that is wholly her own.

nuggets.imageCaption: Lana Del Rey, whose cinematic pop was a clear influence on Eilish.

artistSummary: Billie Eilish and her brother Finneas O'Connell redefined pop from a bedroom in Los Angeles, proving that a DIY ethos could lead to global domination. Their music merges avant-garde electronics and trap with intimate, often dark lyricism, creating a sound that's entirely their own.
